candidate_policy.operator_decision: do_not_pin_candidate; use latest machine-readable market/runtime information dynamically
selection_sources_before_bb: latest existing runtime Cost Gate artifacts
read-only DB/audit artifacts when allowed by PM/E3 scope
forbidden_before_bb: new public Bybit market data call
candidate_policy.if_latest_candidate_differs: ROTATED; do not materialize this NEAR Buy preview; regenerate the candidate-aligned chain for the latest selected candidate

allowed: verify final Mac/origin/Linux source heads are stable and Linux worktree is clean
re-read latest machine-readable runtime/no-authority artifacts and verify the current selected candidate before consuming any snapshot candidate artifacts
review producer cap-lineage repair and focused test evidence
review candidate-aligned standing_demo_operator_authorization_v1 preview only if the latest selected candidate still matches the snapshot candidate
approve or reject materializing only the latest-selected candidate-aligned standing Demo loss-control envelope to runtime with mode 0600
approve or reject no-order false-negative operator review and bounded-probe preflight refresh after materialization for the latest selected candidate
review whether source/runtime admission propagation is sufficient for a later BB-reviewed exchange-facing bounded Demo final window
forbidden: setting bounded-probe operator authorization decision to authorize
Cost Gate lowering or global threshold mutation

acceptance_for_e3_approval: all artifact sha256 values match this request
E3 re-reads latest machine-readable runtime/no-authority artifacts and does not treat the snapshot candidate as manually pinned
the NEAR Buy snapshot artifacts are consumable only if the latest selected candidate still resolves to ma_crossover|NEARUSDT|Buy
if the latest selected candidate differs, E3 returns ROTATED and PM regenerates the candidate-aligned chain for the latest selected candidate
old runtime standing authorization is not consumed as current-candidate authority
candidate-aligned preview remains Demo-only, scoped, unmaterialized before E3 approval, and grants no order/probe by itself
GUI-backed Rust RiskConfig cap lineage is preserved and local 10 USDT is not treated as global risk authority
bounded-probe operator authorization remains defer unless a later exact exchange-facing scope passes E3 and BB
source/runtime heads are stable at E3 consumption time

rejection_to_work_item_mapping.cap_lineage_rejected: repair standing_demo_loss_control_envelope_review cap lineage producer or GUI/equity source contract
rejection_to_work_item_mapping.artifact_stale: rerun no-authority producer chain from latest runtime artifacts
rejection_to_work_item_mapping.candidate_rotated: ROTATED; reselect from latest machine-readable market/runtime artifacts and regenerate the candidate-aligned no-authority chain
rejection_to_work_item_mapping.source_drift: ROTATED; refetch, resync, and regenerate exact-scope request
rejection_to_work_item_mapping.materialization_boundary_too_broad: split standing-envelope materialization from exchange-facing bounded Demo final window
rejection_to_work_item_mapping.runtime_admission_not_reviewable: produce source/runtime admission propagation packet without enabling runtime adapter

next_after_e3.if_approved_and_exchange_facing_scope_ne: open BB exact scope before any public/private Bybit, Decision Lease, bounded Demo final window, order, or probe
next_after_e3.if_rejected: convert E3 rejection reason into the mapped engineering work item unless it is a hard boundary
